target.name: PsiQuantum
target.founder: Jeremy O'Brien, Terry Rudolph, Peter Shadbolt, Mark Thompson

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $1.5B - $23.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.2x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
PsiQuantum, a private-stage quantum computing semiconductor company founded by O'Brien, Rudolph, Shadbolt, and Thompson, carries a current valuation of $7.0B against $1.415B in total capital raised. The valuation assessment draws from a peer universe of 8 private companies in Chips & Semiconductors at similar valuation tiers, supplemented by reference to 3 public semiconductor comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD).

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach here. The 8-company private peer set, including Cerebras ($23.0B), Etched ($5.0B), SambaNova Systems ($5.0B), Credo Semiconductor ($5.0B), and Tenstorrent ($2.7B), establishes a median valuation of $3.9B with a range of $1.5B to $23.0B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (sector median 24.2x across 3 tickers) cannot be applied absent disclosed revenue figures, which private companies typically withhold. The peer-median method alone supports an implied valuation of $3.9B.

The quantitative analysis triangulates to a range of $3.3B (low) to $4.4B (high), with a median of $3.9B. The current $7.0B valuation sits substantially above this peer-derived band. Key uncertainties include the heterogeneity within the peer set—Cerebras at $23.0B represents a significant outlier—and the absence of revenue or growth-rate data, which would anchor relative valuation more tightly. The gap between current valuation and peer-median suggests either material non-public performance factors or valuation premium risk.
